title: bobea elatior
intro: Bobea elatior, also known as Hawaiian haw, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to Hawaii. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Bobea elatior include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat respiratory infections, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Bobea elatior is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications, such as salves and creams, to harness its medicinal properties.
